Idaho · State Licensing Reference

Dietitian
Licensed Dietitian (LD) Guide

Issued by the Dietetic Licensure Board under the Idaho Division of Occupational and Professional Licenses (DOPL). Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$100
$100 initial application fee
Renewal
$120
Every 2 years (transitions from annual $60 to biennial $120 starting mid-2025)
Processing Time
3 to 4 Weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
2 Years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Dietetic Licensure Board

Mailing Address

P.O. Box 83720
Boise, ID 83720-0063

Physical Address

11341 W Chinden Blvd, Bldg 4
Boise, ID 83714

Phone & Fax

Phone: (208) 334-3233

Fax: (208) 327-7005

Email

Credential Categories

Idaho Dietitian License Types

Licensed Dietitian (LD)

The credential to practice dietetics in Idaho, regulated by the Idaho Dietetic Licensure Board under DOPL's Board of Medicine.

Provisional Dietitian

Available for applicants who have completed their education and supervised practice but have not yet passed the CDR exam. Valid for one year, costs $50, and allows practice under the supervision of a Licensed Dietitian.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Idaho Licensed Dietitian (LD)

Complete Education & Supervised Practice

Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 1,000 hours of board-approved supervised practice through an accredited dietitian internship.

Pass the CDR Examination

Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians. Verification of current RD status satisfies exam requirements.

Submit the Application to DOPL

Complete the application form online or via paper. Have it notarized, and submit it with the $100 fee to DOPL.

Provide Verification Documents

Submit a copy of your active CDR card or ensure verification is sent directly. A current CDR card generally confirms both education and examination completion.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Notarized ApplicationApplication form with your notarized signature (if using paper form).
  • CDR Registration CardProof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
  • Official College TranscriptsSent directly from the university showing your conferred degree (if not using CDR as sole verification).
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules

Idaho offers a Provisional License for $50 for individuals who have fulfilled the educational and supervised practice requirements but have not yet passed the CDR exam. A Provisional Licensed Dietitian must practice under the direction and supervision of an Idaho Licensed Dietitian. The provisional license is valid for one year.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Idaho allows for licensure by endorsement. Out-of-state applicants with a current, valid license from a state with equivalent standards or those holding active CDR registration can apply by submitting the standard application, fee, and proof of out-of-state licensure.

Frequently Asked Questions

Idaho Dietitian FAQ

How much does an Idaho dietitian license cost?

The initial license application fee is $100. A provisional license costs $50.

When does the Idaho license need to be renewed?

Starting in mid-2025, Idaho dietitian licenses transition to a biennial (two-year) renewal cycle based on the licensee's birth year. The biennial renewal fee is $120.

Is the Dietitian Licensure Compact active in Idaho?

As of early 2026, Idaho has pending legislation (S1284) to adopt the Dietitian Licensure Compact. If enacted, it would allow eligible dietitians to practice across state lines with compact privilege.
